registry.title: Key Vault network ACL set to Allow for CI/CD pipeline access
registry.justification: GitHub Actions runners use dynamic ephemeral IPs from Azure-hosted pools. These IPs cannot be statically allowlisted in Key Vault firewall rules. The Terraform apply job requires data-plane access to Key Vault secrets during refresh. Compensating control: RBAC-only authorization (enable_rbac_authorization=true) ensures only the CI service principal with Key Vault Secrets Officer role can access secrets. No anonymous or unauthenticated access is possible.
compensating_controls: RBAC authorization enforced (enable_rbac_authorization = true)
Purge protection enabled
Soft delete 7 days enabled
All access audited via diagnostic settings to Log Analytics
TruffleHog scans every PR for secret leakage
registry.remediation_path: Implement OIDC federation for GitHub Actions to Azure (workload identity) — eliminates need for static IP allowlist.
registry.audit_notes: Key Vault firewall was originally Deny. Changed to Allow on 2026-03-22 after repeated 403 ForbiddenByFirewall errors in CI. RBAC remains the primary access control. Azure Policy exemption registered 2026-03-22.

registry.title: Storage Account network rules omitted — Function App Consumption plan constraint
registry.justification: Azure Function App on Consumption plan (Y1 SKU) uses shared Azure infrastructure and does not support VNet injection. Enabling network_rules with default_action=Deny blocks the Function App runtime from accessing the storage account, which is required for host coordination, blob triggers, and file share mounting. This is a documented Azure platform limitation for Y1 Consumption plans. AVD-AZU-0008 (storage firewall) and AVD-AZU-0011 (trusted Azure services bypass) are also covered by this exception — both are direct tfsec equivalents of CKV_AZURE_35/CKV_AZURE_59.
compensating_controls: HTTPS-only traffic enforced (https_traffic_only_enabled = true)
Minimum TLS 1.2 enforced
Public blob access disabled (allow_nested_items_to_be_public = false)
Storage access key stored in Key Vault (not in app settings)
Blob soft delete 7 days enabled
Shared access key required by Function App runtime — cannot be disabled
registry.remediation_path: Upgrade to Function App Premium plan (EP1) to enable VNet integration and storage firewall. Cost: ~$150/month. Not justified for dev portfolio project.
registry.audit_notes: Storage account previously had network_rules Deny which caused 403 errors on azurerm_storage_share resource during Terraform apply. Removed on 2026-03-22. Azure platform limitation confirmed in Microsoft docs. AVD-AZU-0008 and AVD-AZU-0011 added 2026-03-26 to cover tfsec findings for same network access constraint.

registry.title: Storage Account shared key access enabled — Function App Consumption plan runtime requirement
registry.justification: Azure Function App on Y1 Consumption plan requires shared key access to the storage account for host coordination, state management, blob trigger bindings, and file share mounting (host.json, azure-webjobs-* containers). Disabling shared key access breaks the Function App runtime entirely. This is a documented Azure platform limitation: shared key cannot be disabled when a Function App on Consumption plan uses the storage account as its backing store.
compensating_controls: Storage access key stored in Azure Key Vault (not hardcoded)
HTTPS-only traffic enforced (https_traffic_only_enabled = true)
Minimum TLS 1.2 enforced
Public blob access disabled (allow_nested_items_to_be_public = false)
All access logged via storage account diagnostic settings (operational)
Key rotation policy enforced via Key Vault
registry.remediation_path: Migrate to managed identity-based access when Azure Function App Consumption plan gains support for storage-account-level RBAC-only authentication.
registry.audit_notes: Shared key is required by Azure Function App runtime on Y1 plan. Cannot be disabled without breaking the application. Compensating control: key stored in Key Vault, not exposed in configuration. Azure Policy exemption registered 2026-03-25. Previously EXC-008.

registry.title: Storage Account diagnostic logging not configured in Terraform module — PoC scope limitation
registry.justification: The azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting resource for the storage account blob service is outside the scope of this gold-tier Terraform module. Diagnostic settings are managed as a separate operational concern in the monitoring module to avoid coupling infrastructure modules to Log Analytics workspace dependencies. This PoC does not include a monitoring module; diagnostic settings are configured manually in Azure.
compensating_controls: Azure Storage Account diagnostic settings enabled manually in Azure portal
StorageRead, StorageWrite, StorageDelete log categories enabled
Logs retained in Log Analytics workspace for 30 days
Azure Monitor alerts configured for unauthorized access patterns
registry.remediation_path: Create a monitoring Terraform module that manages azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting resources for all gold-tier modules. Reference Log Analytics workspace via data source to decouple from infrastructure modules.
registry.audit_notes: Diagnostic settings are configured outside Terraform scope for this PoC. Logging is active in Azure but not IaC-managed. No Azure Policy exemption needed — MCSB-LT-3 has no built-in policy assignment in this subscription. Previously EXC-009.
